mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the annualized inflation rate measured by CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, central bank tightening expectations rise sharply, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL annualized change exceeds 3.0%, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate top-line growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal produces negative 20-day returns when either series decelerates, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces corporate profit expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following two consecutive months of INDPRO decline, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.
